title: The 30% Raise That Vanished
content: Got a raise once. Thirty percent more. Within six months, I was broke again. Lifestyle creep is real. Your expenses expand to match your income unless you're intentional. Now every raise gets split: half to savings, half to life.
extended_content: It was so subtle. A nicer apartment. Eating out more. Upgrading things I didn't need to upgrade. I didn't feel like I was overspending - I felt like I was 'living to my new income.' But when I looked at my savings six months later... nothing. The same nothing as before the raise. That's when I learned: raises are dangerous if you don't have a plan for them.
when_to_tell: when someone mentions getting a raise
lesson: Plan for raises before you get them
